---
title: "Ecosystem"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
toc-location: left
---

The goal is a modular family of packages rather than one package that does
everything. Each package should have a narrow scientific responsibility and a
clear data handoff.

## Package roles

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
### sagui

Photometric segmentation of broadband or medium-band image cubes. The main
science product is a segmentation map and flux-conserving regional photometry.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### saguiSED

SED fitting for the regional photometry exported by `sagui`. It handles filter
sets, optional backend calls, SED-fit plots, physical-property maps, and
post-fit map regularization.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### capivara

Spectral segmentation and IFU-oriented workflows. Spectral fitting belongs in
`capivara` or in a capivara-specific companion package, not in `saguiSED`.
:::
:::

## Why separate packages?

Keeping SED fitting outside `sagui` avoids forcing users to install heavy Python
dependencies when they only need segmentation and regional photometry. It also
makes it easier to add or replace SED-fitting backends without changing the core
segmentation code.

The same philosophy should hold for future packages:

- segmentation packages define regions and extract fluxes
- fitting packages infer physical quantities from those fluxes
- visualization packages can build paper figures from saved products
- survey-specific packages can encode filter sets and data-release conventions

## Data handoff

The handoff between packages should be boring and explicit: CSV tables for
regional photometry and FITS files for image-plane maps.

```r
sed <- as_sagui_sed_table(
  "region_seds_wide.csv",
  filter_set = lsst_filter_set(),
  unit = "nJy",
  redshift = 0.2
)
```

This keeps downstream SED fitting reproducible even when the segmentation
package changes.

## Regularization layer

Graph-Laplacian smoothing is implemented as a post-fit map estimator in
`saguiSED`, because it acts on region-level physical properties rather than on
the original image cube. The independent backend summaries remain available and
should be retained for tables and uncertainty checks.

```r
maps_smooth <- smooth_sed_property_maps(
  labels = seg$cluster_map,
  fit = fit,
  lambda = 3,
  adjacency = "queen"
)
```

For the paper, this distinction matters: the SED fitting is independent by
region; the smoothed maps are spatially regularized summaries of those
independent fits.
